jokes.text: I've never seen a supernova blow up... But if it's like my Chevy Nova, it'll light up the sky.
jokes.setup: Character expressing wonder about witnessing a supernova
jokes.punchline: Comparing it to his unreliable car that would 'light up the sky' (presumably by catching fire)
jokes.explanation: Classic Fry - takes something grand and cosmic and brings it down to his mundane, blue-collar experience. Perfect character voice and good structure.

jokes.text: What smells like blue?
jokes.setup: Strange smell in the air from the time travel incident
jokes.punchline: Synesthetic description of smell as having a color
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist line that's become iconic. Perfect economy, completely in-character for Fry's confused perception of reality.

jokes.text: Which crazy thing happening are you guys screaming about?
jokes.setup: Multiple catastrophic events happening simultaneously
jokes.punchline: Fry's casual assumption that there are multiple 'crazy things' to choose from
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Fry's obliviousness to crisis, but fairly standard structure.

jokes.text: My popcorn's done. It's less popped than ever.
jokes.setup: Time travel has reversed things, everyone is confused
jokes.punchline: The popcorn has somehow become 'less popped' - logically impossible but fits the reversal theme
jokes.explanation: Clever absurdist joke that reinforces the time reversal concept while being economical.

jokes.text: What's this layer of ozone? That's never been there before.
jokes.setup: Characters noticing differences in 1947
jokes.punchline: Ozone layer existing is the 'strange' thing (from future perspective where it's depleted)
jokes.explanation: Smart environmental joke that works better on rewatch when you realize the irony.

jokes.text: Those cost more lives than they save.
jokes.setup: Being told to put on seat belts for safety
jokes.punchline: Bender's contrarian dismissal of safety measures
jokes.explanation: Very in-character for Bender but not particularly original or clever.

jokes.text: Here's the plan. Zoidberg, pick up the pieces. Everyone else, take five.
jokes.setup: Need to organize post-crash recovery
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg gets all the work while everyone else relaxes
jokes.explanation: Standard 'Zoidberg gets the bad deal' joke but well-executed.

jokes.text: Zoidberg picks up another piece. The crowd goes wild.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg doing mundane cleanup work
jokes.punchline: Treating it like a sporting event with commentary
jokes.explanation: Good Zoidberg moment with nice absurdist sports commentary angle.

jokes.text: In all my years of covering top-Secret discoveries with sheets... I've never dramatically revealed anything this shocking. Dun-Dun-Dun!
jokes.setup: Military officer about to reveal crashed ship
jokes.punchline: Self-aware reference to the dramatic reveal trope, complete with sound effect
jokes.explanation: Nice meta-humor that acknowledges the show's own dramatic conventions.

jokes.text: Which explains why the chronograph turned into this calendar.
jokes.setup: Professor explaining they've traveled to 1947 using his precision digital chronograph
jokes.punchline: The 'precision' device has literally transformed into a simple paper calendar
jokes.explanation: Excellent visual gag that subverts expectations about futuristic technology.

jokes.text: Some idiot must have put metal in the microwave. Yo.
jokes.setup: Professor explaining what caused the time travel accident
jokes.punchline: Bender immediately and casually admits guilt
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing and character work. Bender's casual 'Yo' is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: 'Gym Renovations on Schedule'? What a load.
jokes.setup: Looking at 1947 newspaper headlines
jokes.punchline: Leela dismisses the mundane headline as unbelievable
jokes.explanation: Mild joke about the reliability of construction schedules, nothing special.

jokes.text: That's no flying saucer. That's my ass.
jokes.setup: Newspaper headline about captured flying saucer
jokes.punchline: Bender's crude but accurate description of his body part
jokes.explanation: Very Bender line but relies on crude humor rather than cleverness.

jokes.text: Hello. Ugh. What are you guys doing tonight? I'm up for whatever.
jokes.setup: Revealing that Zoidberg was the 'alien' captured
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg's oblivious casual greeting and social availability despite being dissected
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zoidberg - completely missing the gravity of his situation and just wanting to socialize.

jokes.text: We tore the universe a new space hole. But it's clenching shut fast.
jokes.setup: Explaining the cosmic tear they created
jokes.punchline: Using crude anatomical metaphor for a space-time rift
jokes.explanation: Clever metaphor that makes cosmic phenomena relatable through crude humor.

jokes.text: Don't do anything that affects anything, unless you were supposed to, in which case, don't not do it.
jokes.setup: Giving time travel safety instructions
jokes.punchline: Logically circular and impossible-to-follow instruction
jokes.explanation: Brilliant paradoxical instruction that captures the logical impossibility of time travel rules.

jokes.text: But existing is basically all I do!
jokes.setup: Being warned he might cease to exist
jokes.punchline: Fry's simple but profound observation about his life's lack of purpose
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Fry's existential simplicity and becomes unexpectedly profound.

jokes.text: Not a day goes by I don't ask myself that.
jokes.setup: Being asked why he came to Earth
jokes.punchline: Fry's honest admission of existential confusion
jokes.explanation: Good character moment that shows Fry's deeper confusion about his place in life.

jokes.text: I want that toilet so clean I could eat off it. Because I intend to.
jokes.setup: Military sergeant demanding clean toilets using common expression
jokes.punchline: Reveals he literally means to eat off the toilet
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of a common phrase but relies on gross-out humor.

jokes.text: Then I am lucky you knocked me onto this pile of rusty bayonets.
jokes.setup: Fry 'saving' his grandfather from being hit by a jeep
jokes.punchline: The 'rescue' landed him in an equally dangerous situation
jokes.explanation: Good ironic reversal showing how Fry's help often makes things worse.

jokes.text: I could feel myself fading away, like Greg Kinnear.
jokes.setup: Describing the sensation of potentially ceasing to exist
jokes.punchline: Comparing existential crisis to a fading actor's career
jokes.explanation: Clever pop culture reference that works on multiple levels but is somewhat dated.

jokes.text: Handling raw chicken? Best part of the job. Finger-Licking good.
jokes.setup: Enos preparing to handle raw chicken for meal prep
jokes.punchline: Using KFC slogan while talking about unsafe food handling
jokes.explanation: Simple gross-out humor with brand reference, not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Yo, Homes, we need a microwave.
jokes.setup: Leela trying to blend in with 1940s slang
jokes.punchline: Using completely anachronistic modern urban slang instead
jokes.explanation: Good fish-out-of-water humor showing Leela's attempt to fit in backfiring.

jokes.text: Yo, Homes, we need a microwave.
jokes.setup: Fry trying to blend in with 1947 culture
jokes.punchline: Using completely anachronistic slang
jokes.explanation: Mild humor about Fry's cultural confusion. The anachronism is expected but fits character.

jokes.text: And a foot-Soaking tub, since, as a woman, you'll be in front of it all day.
jokes.setup: Salesman describing features of 1940s oven
jokes.punchline: Casually sexist assumption about women's roles
jokes.explanation: Period-accurate sexism played for uncomfortable laughs, works as social commentary.

jokes.text: She's hysterical, so I'll talk to you.
jokes.setup: Leela asking about microwaves
jokes.punchline: Salesman dismissing her as 'hysterical' and turning to Fry instead
jokes.explanation: Continuation of period sexism theme, predictable but serves the historical setting.

jokes.text: Oh. Women.
jokes.setup: Witnessing the salesman's sexist behavior
jokes.punchline: Fry's casual acceptance and eye-roll at sexism
jokes.explanation: Weak payoff to the sexism setup, Fry's casual misogyny isn't particularly funny.

jokes.text: Hey, that's my brain!
jokes.setup: Enos opening his lunch box containing 'snacks'
jokes.punchline: The 'snacks' are actually Fry's brain, revealing the temporal loop
jokes.explanation: Excellent reveal that shows the complex time paradox in a simple, shocking way.

jokes.text: That's my fiancée, Mildred. Grandma Mildred? No dessert.
jokes.setup: Enos expressing interest in a waitress
jokes.punchline: Fry realizing it's his grandmother and awkwardly shutting down grandfather's romantic interest
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the incest subplot, creates appropriate cringe while advancing plot.

jokes.text: You almost got neutered. Ain't as bad as killed. For me it is.
jokes.setup: Enos downplaying injury to his groin area
jokes.punchline: Fry's panic because that would prevent his own existence
jokes.explanation: Good character-driven humor that reinforces the existential stakes for Fry.

jokes.text: I'll have soylent green... With a soylent orange and soylent coleslaw.
jokes.setup: Characters trying to order food in 1947
jokes.punchline: Ordering futuristic fictional food that doesn't exist yet
jokes.explanation: Simple fish-out-of-water joke with sci-fi reference, functional but not exceptional.

jokes.text: I'll have a croque monsieur, paella, two mutton pills and a stein of mead.
jokes.setup: Ordering food in 1947 diner
jokes.punchline: Mix of fancy European food and futuristic/medieval items
jokes.explanation: Weaker version of the same fish-out-of-water premise.

jokes.text: Oh, lord! We'll have to endure the horrible music of the Big Bopper... And then the terrible tragedy of his death.
jokes.setup: Being stuck in the 1940s
jokes.punchline: Treating both the music and the musician's death as equally tragic
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that treats aesthetic displeasure and death as equivalent tragedies.

jokes.text: Well, I killed my grandfather. Wait, why do you still exist? I don't know. Maybe God loves me.
jokes.setup: Fry casually admitting to killing his grandfather
jokes.punchline: His innocent explanation for surviving a logical paradox
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry line - combines his obliviousness with unexpected theological simplicity.

jokes.text: Well, I killed my grandfather. Wha...? Wait, why do you still exist? I don't know. Maybe God loves me.
jokes.setup: Time travel paradox - killing grandfather should erase existence
jokes.punchline: Fry's naive theological explanation for logical impossibility
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of time travel logic with perfectly innocent Fry explanation. Sets up the bigger reveal beautifully.

jokes.text: If it makes you feel any better... He was vaporized, so he can't come back as a zombie.
jokes.setup: Trying to comfort Mildred about Enos's death
jokes.punchline: Offering zombie-prevention as consolation
jokes.explanation: Perfectly inappropriate comfort that's both darkly funny and very Professor-like.

jokes.text: Where we're building the fake moon landing? We'll have to really land on the moon. Invent NASA and tell them.
jokes.setup: Mention of Area 51
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to moon landing conspiracy, then having to actually do it
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of moon landing conspiracy theory - they have to actually do it.

jokes.text: Are you coming on to me? I take exception to that.
jokes.setup: Scientist asking about alien-human hybrid plans
jokes.punchline: Bender interpreting scientific question as sexual advance
jokes.explanation: Good Bender moment - sexual misinterpretation fits his character. The indignation is perfectly delivered.

jokes.text: How about these cookies, sugar?
jokes.setup: Mildred offering to bake sugar cookies
jokes.punchline: The double entendre of 'sugar' as both ingredient and term of endearment with seductive implication
jokes.explanation: Simple double entendre that sets up the seduction scene.

jokes.text: What the hell have you done?! Relax. She can't be my grandmother. I figured it out.
jokes.setup: Professor discovering Fry in bed with Mildred
jokes.punchline: Fry's confident but wrong logic about avoiding paradox
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing Fry's flawed reasoning and the Professor's horror.

jokes.text: Isn't it obvious? You are.
jokes.setup: Fry asking who his grandfather is if Enos is dead
jokes.punchline: The shocking revelation that Fry is his own grandfather
jokes.explanation: The central revelation of the episode - brilliantly executed time paradox that recontextualizes everything.

jokes.text: Come on, lighten up. What is this, a funeral?
jokes.setup: Military scientists conducting serious autopsy on Zoidberg
jokes.punchline: Bender's inappropriate joke while literally being dissected
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender inappropriateness, though the setup is somewhat predictable.

jokes.text: The same deviled egg.
jokes.setup: Finding a deviled egg in Bender's stomach
jokes.punchline: Realizing it's the same egg from multiple time loops
jokes.explanation: Clever time paradox joke that rewards attention to detail.

jokes.text: A lesson in not changing history from Mr. I'm-My-Own-Grandpa.
jokes.setup: Professor warning about changing history
jokes.punchline: Leela pointing out the hypocrisy given Fry's situation
jokes.explanation: Great callback that highlights the absurdity and creates a memorable phrase.

jokes.text: Great Roosevelt's ghost! Hi-Ya! Ya! Ya! Take this, Mr. President. Sir.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg bursting into the room with the President
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg's martial arts sounds followed by accidentally attacking Truman
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy moment with Zoidberg's characteristic incompetence.

jokes.text: Choke on that, causality!
jokes.setup: Successfully escaping despite time travel complications
jokes.punchline: Bender's triumphant declaration against the laws of physics
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Bender line that personifies abstract concepts - very quotable and in-character.

jokes.text: Oh, poor Bender. He must be so lonely right now, trapped 1000 years in the past.
jokes.setup: Expressing sympathy for Bender being left behind
jokes.punchline: Sets up the reveal that he's been there all along
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the final reveal, creates false pathos before the punchline.

jokes.text: What was it like lying in that hole for 1000 years? I was enjoying it until you showed up.
jokes.setup: Asking Bender about his millennium-long wait
jokes.punchline: Bender's characteristic antisocial response
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender response - antisocial and ungrateful despite being rescued.

jokes.text: If history doesn't care that our degenerate friend, Fry... Is his own grandfather, who are we to judge?
jokes.setup: Wrapping up the time paradox implications
jokes.punchline: Casual acceptance of impossible situation with moral relativism
jokes.explanation: Nice philosophical wrap-up that treats the absurd situation as normal, classic Professor.
